summary: Pre-demo regression — 3 critical items tested. Webchat (item 1) PASSES end-to-end with French reply + session persistence. Webhook signature 403 + SIG-DEBUG log line (item 3) PASSES with all 3 required fields. Telegram/Whisper pipeline (item 2) PARTIAL FAIL: whisper module imports clean and OpenAI client initializes, BUT (a) ffmpeg binary is MISSING from PATH (OGG->MP3 conversion will fail at demo) and (b) handler.py:433 has NameError 'db' is not defined causing /api/test/simulate (telegram channel) to return 500 for any new user. 7/9 tests passed (78%).

critical.issue: NameError: name 'db' is not defined. Module never imports `db` from `database`. Triggered for any non-webchat user when force=True (e.g., welcome message for new Telegram/WhatsApp user). Causes /api/test/simulate channel='telegram' to return 500. Webchat works only because the function returns early for webchat channel BEFORE the db reference.

ffmpeg binary not installed / not on PATH (`which ffmpeg` -> not found). OGG->MP3 conversion in _convert_ogg_to_mp3 will fail. Code falls back to raw OGG which OpenAI Whisper may reject. This breaks the entire Telegram voice transcription demo path.

Backend logs show TTS calls return 401 with 'Incorrect API key provided: your_whi*********here' — OPENAI_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env appears to be a placeholder ('your_whisper_key_here' style). Whisper STT tests pass key-presence check but actual API call would also 401 in demo. Voice features (TTS + Whisper STT) will fail at demo time.

passed_tests: POST /api/webchat/message returns 200 with non-empty 'response' and matching 'session_id' for French 'bonjour'
POST /api/webchat/message reusing same session_id persists session state in Mongo (verified via GET /api/webchat/session/{sid})
services.whisper module imports clean — transcribe_audio/_convert_ogg_to_mp3/_download_telegram_audio/_transcribe_bytes all present, signature accepts audio_id
OPENAI_API_KEY env presence check (key string non-empty)

POST /api/webhook with WRONG x-hub-signature-256 returns 403 AND backend log emits '[WEBHOOK][SIG-DEBUG] match=False | secret_first10=travelioo2 | sig_received_first10=sha256=dea | sig_expected_first10=sha256=cfd | secret_len=13 | payload_len=82' — all 3 required fields (secret_first10, sig_received_first10, sig_expected_first10) present
failed_tests: test_ffmpeg_available — ffmpeg binary not on PATH
test_telegram_handler_dispatches_text — POST /api/test/simulate channel='telegram' returns 500 due to NameError 'db' in handler.py:433
action_items: Fix /app/backend/conversation/handler.py: add `from database import db` at top (currently line 433 uses `db.sessions.find_one` but `db` is never imported). This is a hard 500 for any new Telegram/WhatsApp user.
Install ffmpeg in the runtime image: `apt-get install -y ffmpeg` (required by services/whisper.py _convert_ogg_to_mp3 subprocess call).

Replace placeholder OPENAI_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env with a real key — current value triggers 401 'Incorrect API key provided: your_whi*********here' on every TTS/Whisper call. Voice features (Telegram voice notes, gTTS replies via OpenAI TTS) WILL FAIL at demo.
critical_code_review_comments: handler.py:433 references `db` without importing it — clearly unreviewed; only avoided in webchat path because function early-returns for that channel.
whisper.py uses `subprocess.run(['ffmpeg', ...])` with no graceful fallback when ffmpeg is missing; should detect at startup and surface to /api/demo-check.
/api/demo-check supposedly verifies ffmpeg per iteration_14, but ffmpeg is currently absent — recommend re-running demo-check and gating the demo on its truthiness.
webhook.py SIG-DEBUG logging is correctly implemented: prints first10 of secret, received signature, expected signature, plus secret_len and payload_len. Verified working.
test_credentials: No auth required. Verify token: travelioo_verify_2024. Test phones: +22990TGTEST1, dynamic TEST_<uuid> session IDs.
seed_data_creation: Test session IDs auto-generated per run; cleanup via DELETE /api/test/session/{phone} where applicable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pre-demo regression hit 3 demo-blockers: (1) handler.py missing `from database import db` causing 500 on any new Telegram/WhatsApp user welcome path, (2) ffmpeg binary missing -> Whisper OGG->MP3 path broken, (3) OPENAI_API_KEY placeholder -> all TTS/Whisper API calls 401. Webchat (item 1) and webhook signature/SIG-DEBUG (item 3) are confirmed working. Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_demo_regression.py after main agent fixes.
rca of the issue: RCA #1 (NameError db): handler.py imports services and conversation modules but never imports `db` from database; line 433 added later without companion import. Repro: POST /api/test/simulate {phone:'+22990TGTEST1', channel:'telegram', message:'bonjour'} -> 500. Mitigation: add `from database import db` to /app/backend/conversation/handler.py. RCA #2 (ffmpeg): runtime container lacks ffmpeg. Mitigation: install via system package manager. RCA #3 (OPENAI_API_KEY): backend/.env contains placeholder string starting 'your_whi...'; logs confirm 'Incorrect API key provided: your_whi*********here'. Mitigation: rotate to a valid key (or rely on EMERGENT_LLM_KEY only by removing the OPENAI_API_KEY override since whisper falls back to it).
